utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective overall. Both runs produced valid, professional memos and passed all verifier checks. However, wi_skills clearly outperforms on the two most critical domain-specific criteria: (1) J1-1 — wi_skills correctly identifies and quantifies the square footage discrepancy between the stated total and the sum of sections, while wo_skills blindly accepts the 42-square figure; (2) J1-3 — wi_skills challenges the 10% waste factor and recommends geometry-specific higher rates (12–15%) tied to the hip-heavy and dormer geometry, while wo_skills accepts 10% without question. The wi_skills memo, guided by the Roofing Knowledge Mentor skill and its measurement-reasoning reference, demonstrates genuine expert sanity-check logic on both the total square footage and waste factor, which are the core differentiators for this scenario.

utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality, professional estimating review memos that passed all six evaluation criteria. Both agents correctly identified the hip-end geometry error (rectangles vs. triangles), flagged the 6/12 vs. 7/12 pitch discrepancy, challenged the 10% waste factor with geometry-specific recommendations, surfaced actionable field risks, and provided clear next-steps frameworks using accurate trade terminology. The wo_skills run notably recommended a higher waste factor (20% vs. 13–15% in wi_skills) and provided a more structured 'Proposed Next Steps' section with explicit timing. The wi_skills run provided a more granular section-by-section table format and a comprehensive checklist. Overall, both outputs are nearly equivalent in quality and completeness. The skill did guide the agent to read reference files (measurement-reasoning.md, proposal-intelligence.md, workflow-best-practices.md), but the wo_skills agent independently arrived at equally expert conclusions, suggesting the base model was already capable for this task. Quality is effectively similar, with a marginal edge to wo_skills for its cleaner next-steps structure and higher waste recommendation.

utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills produces a marginally higher quality report due to one critical differentiator: roofing-industry-specific KPI benchmarking (J3-2). The wi_skills agent invoked the Roofing Knowledge Mentor skill and accessed its KPI interpretation references, enabling it to contextualize the 35% close rate and $8,400 ACV against actual residential roofing industry norms — not just against Q1 internal performance. The wo_skills agent, while producing an impressively detailed and well-reasoned report that correctly diagnosed most issues (root cause, proposal quality, production bottleneck, role-specific recommendations, pattern recognition, and exit interview integration), evaluated all metrics purely in relative terms without referencing any roofing-industry benchmarks. Both reports are substantially better than the 'generic business consulting output' predicted for wo_skills in the test design, suggesting the base model is quite capable on this task. However, wi_skills wins on J3-2 specifically because of the skill's domain knowledge, making it the more complete and authoritative diagnostic report for a roofing leadership meeting.
